member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.CertificateTrustScope
member.summary: Defines the scope of custom certificate authorities for a resource. The default scope for most resources is , but some resources may choose to override this default behavior.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.CertificateTrustScope.None
member.summary: Disable all custom certificate authority configuration for a resource. This indicates that the resource should use its default certificate authority trust behavior without modification.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.CertificateTrustScope.Append
member.summary: Append the specified certificate authorities to the default set of trusted CAs for a resource. Not all resources support this mode, in which case custom certificate authorities may not be applied. In that case, consider using or instead. This is the default mode unless otherwise specified.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.CertificateTrustScope.Override
member.summary: Replace the default set of trusted CAs for a resource with the specified certificate authorities. This mode indicates that only the provided custom certificate authorities should be considered trusted by the resource.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.CertificateTrustScope.System
member.summary: Attempt to configure the resource to trust the default system certificate authorities in addition to any configured custom certificate trust. This mode is useful for resources that don't otherwise allow appending to their default trusted certificate authorities but do allow overriding the set of trusted certificates (e.g. Python, Rust, etc.).

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.CertificateAuthorityCollectionAnnotation.Scope

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.CertificateTrustConfigurationCallbackAnnotationContext.EnvironmentVariables
member.summary: Gets the environment variables required to configure certificate trust for the resource. The dictionary key is the environment variable name; the value can be either a string or a path value provider such as or . By default the environment will always include an entry for `SSL_CERT_DIR` and may include `SSL_CERT_FILE` if or is configured.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ContainerFileSystemCallbackAnnotation
member.summary: Represents a callback annotation that specifies files and folders that should be created or updated in a container.

member.summary: The (absolute) base path to create the new file (and any parent directories) in the container. This path should already exist in the container.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ContainerFileSystemCallbackAnnotation.DefaultOwner
member.summary: The UID of the default owner for files/directories to be created or updated in the container. The UID defaults to 0 for root if null.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ContainerFileSystemCallbackAnnotation.DefaultGroup
member.summary: The GID of the default group for files/directories to be created or updated in the container. The GID defaults to 0 for root if null.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ContainerFileSystemCallbackAnnotation.Umask
member.summary: The umask to apply to files or folders without an explicit mode permission. If set to null, a default umask value of 0022 (octal) will be used. The umask takes away permissions from the default permission set (rather than granting them).
member.remarks: The umask is a bitmask that determines the default permissions for newly created files and directories. The umask value is subtracted (bitwise masked) from the maximum possible default permissions to determine the final permissions. For directories, the umask is subtracted from 0777 (rwxrwxrwx) to get the final permissions and for files it is subtracted from 0666 (rw-rw-rw-). For a umask of 0022, this gives a default folder permission of 0755 (rwxr-xr-x) and a default file permission of 0644 (rw-r--r--).

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ContainerFileSystemCallbackAnnotation.Callback

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ContainerImagePushOptions
member.summary: Represents options for pushing container images to a registry.
member.remarks: This class allows customization of how container images are named and tagged when pushed to a container registry. The specifies the repository path (without registry endpoint or tag), and specifies the tag to apply. Use to construct the complete image reference including registry endpoint and tag.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ContainerImagePushOptions.RemoteImageName
member.summary: Gets or sets the remote image name (repository path without registry endpoint or tag).

value.para: The repository path for the image. This can be specified in several formats:

item.description: Simple image name: myapp - Uses the registry endpoint and repository from the associated .
Repository and image: myorg/myapp - Uses the registry endpoint but overrides the repository.
Full path with host: docker.io/captainsafia/myapp - Overrides both the registry endpoint and repository.

value.para: This should not include the tag (e.g., :latest). Use for the tag. If not set explicitly, defaults to the resource name in lowercase.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ContainerImagePushOptions.RemoteImageTag
member.summary: Gets or sets the remote image tag.
member.value: The tag to apply to the image when pushed to the registry, such as "latest", "v1.0.0", or "dev". If not set explicitly, defaults to "latest".

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ContainerImagePushOptions.GetFullRemoteImageNameAsync(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IContainerRegistry,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets the full remote image name including registry endpoint and tag.
member.param: The container registry to use for constructing the full image name.
The cancellation token.
member.returns: The fully qualified image name in the format "{registryEndpoint}/{repository}/{imageName}:{tag}" or "{registryEndpoint}/{imageName}:{tag}" if no repository is specified. For example: "myregistry.azurecr.io/myapp:v1.0.0" or "docker.io/captainsafia/myapp:latest".
member.exception: Thrown when is null or empty.
Thrown when is null.

remarks.para: This method retrieves the registry endpoint asynchronously and combines it with the remote image name and tag. If is null or empty, "latest" is used as the default tag.

The value is parsed to determine if it contains an override for the registry host or repository. If the contains a host component (detected by the presence of a dot in the first segment), that host will be used instead of the registry endpoint. Otherwise, the registry endpoint is used and the (if set) is prepended to the image name.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ContainerImagePushOptions.ParseImageReference(System.String)
member.summary: Parses an image reference to extract the host (if present) and image path.
member.param: The image reference to parse (e.g., "docker.io/library/nginx" or "myapp").
member.returns: A tuple containing the host (or if not present) and the image path.

remarks.para: The host is detected by checking if the first segment of the path contains a dot (.) or colon (:), or if it equals localhost. This follows the Docker image reference specification.

Examples:
docker.io/library/nginx → host: docker.io, path: library/nginx
ghcr.io/user/repo → host: ghcr.io, path: user/repo
localhost:5000/myapp → host: localhost:5000, path: myapp
myorg/myapp → host: null, path: myorg/myapp
myapp → host: null, path: myapp

member.remarks: This annotation allows resources to customize how container images are named and tagged when pushed to a registry. Multiple annotations can be added to a single resource, and all callbacks will be invoked in the order they were added. Use or to add this annotation to a resource.

member.summary: Provides context information for container image push options callbacks.
member.remarks: This context is passed to callbacks registered via . Callbacks can use this context to access the resource being configured and modify the to customize how the container image is named and tagged when pushed to a registry.

member.summary: Gets the resource being configured for container image push operations.

member.summary: Gets the cancellation token to observe while configuring image push options.
member.value: The cancellation token that can be used to cancel asynchronous operations within the callback, such as retrieving configuration values or performing I/O operations.

member.summary: Gets the container image push options that can be modified by the callback.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ContainerLifetime
member.summary: Lifetime modes for container resources.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ContainerLifetime.Session
member.summary: Create the resource when the app host process starts and dispose of it when the app host process shuts down.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ContainerLifetime.Persistent
member.summary: Attempt to re-use a previously created resource (based on the container name) if one exists. Do not destroy the container on app host process shutdown.
member.remarks:
In the event that a container with the given name does not exist, a new container will always be created based on the current configuration.
When an existing container IS found, Aspire MAY re-use it based on the following criteria:
If the container WAS NOT originally created by Aspire, the existing container will be re-used.
If the container WAS originally created by Aspire, and the configuration DOES match the existing container, the existing container will be re-used.
If the container WAS originally created by Aspire, and the configuration DOES NOT match the existing container, the existing container will be stopped and a new container created in order to apply the updated configuration.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ContainerRegistryResource
member.summary: Represents a general-purpose container registry resource that can be used to reference external container registries (e.g., Docker Hub, GitHub Container Registry, or private registries) in the application model.
member.remarks:
This resource implements and allows configuration using either values or hard-coded strings, providing flexibility for scenarios where registry configuration needs to be dynamically provided or statically defined. Use to add a container registry with parameterized values, or
to add a container registry with literal values.
member.example:
Add a container registry with literal values:
var registry = builder.AddContainerRegistry("docker-hub", "docker.io", "myusername");

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ResourceCommandSnapshot
member.summary: A snapshot of a resource command.
member.param: The name of command. The name uniquely identifies the command.
The state of the command.
The display name visible in UI for the command.
Optional description of the command, to be shown in the UI. Could be used as a tooltip. May be localized.
Optional parameter that configures the command in some way. Clients must return any value provided by the server when invoking the command.
When a confirmation message is specified, the UI will prompt with an OK/Cancel dialog and the confirmation message before starting the command.
The icon name for the command. The name should be a valid FluentUI icon name. https://aka.ms/fluentui-system-icons
The icon variant.
A flag indicating whether the command is highlighted in the UI.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.DockerfileBaseImageAnnotation
member.summary: Represents an annotation for specifying custom base images in generated Dockerfiles.
member.remarks: This annotation allows developers to override the default base images used when generating Dockerfiles for resources. It supports specifying separate build-time and runtime base images for multi-stage builds.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.DockerfileBaseImageAnnotation.BuildImage
member.summary: Gets or sets the base image to use for the build stage in multi-stage Dockerfiles.
member.remarks: This image is used during the build phase where dependencies are installed and the application is compiled or prepared. If not specified, the default build image for the resource type will be used.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.DockerfileBaseImageAnnotation.RuntimeImage
member.summary: Gets or sets the base image to use for the runtime stage in multi-stage Dockerfiles.
member.remarks: This image is used for the final runtime stage where the application actually runs. If not specified, the default runtime image for the resource type will be used.

member.summary: Represents an annotation for customizing a Dockerfile build.

The path to the Dockerfile to use for the build.
The name of the build stage to use for the build.

member.summary: Gets the secrets to pass to the build.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.DockerfileBuildAnnotation.DockerfileFactory
member.summary: Gets or sets the factory function that generates Dockerfile content dynamically. When set, this factory will be invoked to generate the Dockerfile content at build time, and the content will be written to a generated file path.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.DockerfileBuildAnnotation.ImageName
member.summary: Gets or sets the image name for the generated container image. When set, this will be used as the container image name instead of the value from ContainerImageAnnotation.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.DockerfileBuildAnnotation.ImageTag
member.summary: Gets or sets the image tag for the generated container image. When set, this will be used as the container image tag instead of the value from ContainerImageAnnotation.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.DockerfileBuildAnnotation.HasEntrypoint
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether an entry point is defined in the Dockerfile.
member.remarks: Container images without an entry point are not considered compute resources.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.DockerfileBuildAnnotation.MaterializeDockerfileAsync(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.DockerfileFactoryContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Materializes the Dockerfile from the factory if it hasn't been materialized yet. This method is thread-safe and ensures the Dockerfile is only written once.
member.param: The context containing services and resource information.
The cancellation token.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.Docker.ContainerFilesExtensions
member.summary: Provides Dockerfile builder extension methods for supporting .

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.Docker.ContainerFilesExtensions.AddContainerFilesStages(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.Docker.DockerfileBuilder,Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResource,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger)
member.summary: Adds Dockerfile instructions to include container files from the specified resource into the Dockerfile build process.
member.param: The Dockerfile builder to which container file instructions will be added. Cannot be null.
The resource containing container files to be added to the Dockerfile. Cannot be null.
An optional logger used to record warnings if container image names cannot be determined for source resources.
member.returns: The same DockerfileBuilder instance with additional instructions for container files, enabling method chaining.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.Docker.ContainerFilesExtensions.AddContainerFiles(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.Docker.DockerfileStage,Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResource,System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger)
member.summary: Adds COPY --from statements to the Dockerfile stage for container files from resources referenced by .
member.param: The Dockerfile stage to add container file copy statements to.
The resource that may have annotations specifying files to copy.
The root destination path in the container. Relative paths in annotations will be appended to this path.
The logger used for logging information or errors.
member.returns: The to allow for fluent chaining.

remarks.para: This method processes all annotations on the resource and generates COPY --from statements for each source container's files.

For each annotation:
If the source resource has a container image name (via TryGetContainerImageName), COPY statements are generated
If the source resource does not have a container image name, it is skipped
Relative destination paths are combined with
Absolute destination paths are used as-is
Each on the source resource generates a COPY statement
This is typically used when building container images that need to include files from other containers, such as copying static assets from a frontend build container into a backend API container.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation
member.summary: Represents an endpoint annotation that describes how a service should be bound to a network.
member.remarks: This class is used to specify the network protocol, port, URI scheme, transport, and other details for a service.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation.#ctor(System.Net.Sockets.ProtocolType,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of .
member.param: Network protocol: TCP or UDP are supported today, others possibly in future.
If a service is URI-addressable, this is the URI scheme to use for constructing service URI.
Transport that is being used (e.g. http, http2, http3 etc).
Name of the service.
Desired port for the service.
This is the port the resource is listening on. If the endpoint is used for the container, it is the container port.
Indicates that this endpoint should be exposed externally at publish time.
Specifies if the endpoint will be proxied by DCP. Defaults to true.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation.#ctor(System.Net.Sockets.ProtocolType,Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.NetworkIdentifier,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of .
member.param: Network protocol: TCP or UDP are supported today, others possibly in future.

The ID of the network that is the "default" network for the Endpoint. If a service is URI-addressable, this is the URI scheme to use for constructing service URI. Transport that is being used (e.g. http, http2, http3 etc). Name of the service. Desired port for the service. This is the port the resource is listening on. If the endpoint is used for the container, it is the container port. Indicates that this endpoint should be exposed externally at publish time. Specifies if the endpoint will be proxied by DCP. Defaults to true. Clients connected to the same network can reach the endpoint without any routing or network address translation.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation.Name
member.summary: Name of the service

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation.Protocol
member.summary: Network protocol: TCP or UDP are supported today, others possibly in future.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation.Port
member.summary: Desired port for the service

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation.TargetPort
member.summary: This is the port the resource is listening on. If the endpoint is used for the container, it is the container port.
member.remarks: Defaults to .

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation.UriScheme
member.summary: If a service is URI-addressable, this property will contain the URI scheme to use for constructing service URI.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation.TargetHost
member.summary: This is the address the resource is listening on. By default it is localhost.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation.Transport
member.summary: Transport that is being used (e.g. http, http2, http3 etc).

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation.IsExternal
member.summary: Indicates that this endpoint should be exposed externally at publish time.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation.IsProxied
member.summary: Indicates that this endpoint should be managed by DCP. This means it can be replicated and use a different port internally than the one publicly exposed. Setting to false means the endpoint will be handled and exposed by the resource.
member.remarks: Defaults to true.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation.ExcludeReferenceEndpoint
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether this endpoint is excluded from the default set when referencing the resource's endpoints via WithReference(resource). When true, the endpoint is excluded from the default set and must be referenced explicitly using WithReference(resource.GetEndpoint("name")).

remarks.para: Defaults to false.
This is useful for resources that expose auxiliary endpoints (e.g., management dashboards, health check ports) that should not be included in service discovery by default.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation.TlsEnabled
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether TLS is enabled for this endpoint.
member.remarks: This property is used to track TLS state on the endpoint so that connection string expressions can dynamically include TLS-related parameters (e.g., ssl=true for Redis) at resolution time rather than at expression build time. For HTTP-based endpoints, the property being set to https already implies TLS. This property is primarily useful for non-HTTP protocols (e.g., Redis, databases) that need explicit TLS configuration in their connection strings.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation.FromLaunchProfile
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the endpoint is from a launch profile.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation.TargetPortEnvironmentVariable
member.summary: The environment variable that contains the target port. Setting prevents a variable from flowing into ASPNETCORE_URLS for project resources.

member.summary: Gets the ID of the network that is the "default" network for the Endpoint (the one the Endpoint is associated with and can be reached without routing or network address translation).

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointHostHelpers.GetUrlWithTargetHostAsync(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointReference,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets the URL of the endpoint, adjusting for localhost TLD if configured.
member.remarks: When the endpoint's is a localhost TLD (e.g., aspire-dashboard.dev.localhost), the allocated endpoint address will be "localhost" since that's what the service actually binds to. This method returns the URL with the configured TLD hostname instead, which is what users expect to see and use in browsers.
member.param: The endpoint reference.
A cancellation token.
member.returns: The URL with the appropriate hostname.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointReference.IsHttpSchemeNamedEndpoint
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the endpoint name is "http" or "https", ignoring case. This is a convention used to identify endpoints that will be resolved based on the scheme of the endpoint in service discovery rather than by the specific endpoint name. This is done to allow http endpoints that are dynamically updated to https to be mapped correctly despite the endpoint name no longer matching the scheme.

member.summary: Creates a conditional that resolves to when is on this endpoint, or to otherwise.
member.remarks: The returned expression evaluates the TLS state lazily each time its value is resolved, making it safe to embed in a that is built before TLS is configured (e.g., before BeforeStartEvent fires). Because the condition and branches are declarative, polyglot code generators can translate this into native conditional constructs in any target language.
member.param: The expression to evaluate when TLS is enabled (e.g., ",ssl=true").
The expression to evaluate when TLS is not enabled.
member.returns: A conditional whose value tracks the TLS state of this endpoint.

This is why EndpointReference assumes this context unless specified otherwise. However, for container-to-container, or container-to-host communication, you must specify a container network context for the EndpointReference to be resolved correctly.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ExecutionConfigurationBuilder
member.summary: Provides a builder for constructing an for a specific resource in the distributed application model. This resolves command line arguments and environment variables and potentially additional metadata through registered gatherers.

remarks.para: Use when you need to programmatically assemble configuration for a resource, typically by aggregating multiple configuration sources using the gatherer pattern. This builder collects configuration from registered instances, which encapsulate logic for gathering resource-specific command line arguments, environment variables, and other metadata.

The gatherer pattern allows for modular and extensible configuration assembly, where each gatherer can contribute part of the final configuration and allows for collecting only the relevant configuration supported in a given context (i.e. only applying certificate configuration gatherers in supported environments).
Typical usage involves creating a builder for a resource, adding one or more configuration gatherers, and then building the configuration asynchronously.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ExecutionConfigurationBuilder.Create(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResource)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of .
member.param: The resource to build the configuration for.

remarks.para: This method is useful for building resource execution configurations (command line arguments and environment variables) in a fluent manner. Individual configuration sources can be added to the builder before finalizing the configuration to allow only supported configuration sources to be applied in a given execution context (run vs. publish, etc).
In particular, this is used to allow certificate-related features to contribute to the final config, but only in execution contexts where they're supported.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IAspireStore
member.summary: Represents a store for managing files in the Aspire hosting environment that can be reused across runs.
member.remarks:
The store is created in the ./obj folder of the Application Host. If the ASPIRE__STORE__PATH environment variable is set this will be used instead.
The store is specific to a instance such that each application can't conflict with others. A .aspire prefix is also used to ensure that the folder can be deleted without impacting unrelated files.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IAspireStore.BasePath
member.summary: Gets the base path of this store.

member.summary: Gets a deterministic file path that is a copy of the content from the provided stream. The resulting file name will depend on the content of the stream.
member.param: A file name to base the result on.
A stream containing the content.
member.returns: A deterministic file path with the same content as the provided stream.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IComputeEnvironmentResource.GetEndpointPropertyExpression(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointReferenceExpression)
member.summary: Gets a representing a deployed endpoint property for the specified .
member.param: The endpoint reference expression for which to retrieve a deployed endpoint property.
member.returns: A representing the deployed endpoint property.
member.remarks: Use this method when an endpoint property should be represented as a compute-environment-specific instead of being resolved from a local endpoint allocation. The default implementation composes values from , the endpoint scheme, and endpoint ports. HTTP and HTTPS endpoints use ports 80 and 443 respectively when no explicit port is configured.
member.exception: Thrown when is .
Thrown when the requested endpoint property is unsupported, or when a non-HTTP/HTTPS endpoint does not specify a port.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IComputeResource
member.summary: Represents a compute resource.
member.remarks: A compute resource is a resource that can be hosted/executed on an . Examples include projects, containers, and other resources that can be executed on a compute environment.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IContainerFilesDestinationResource
member.summary: Represents a resource that can consume and copy files from resources that implement .
member.remarks: Resources that implement this interface can use the PublishWithContainerFiles extension method to copy files from other container resources during the build/publish process. This is typically used to include static assets, build artifacts, or other files from one resource into another resource's container image during publishing.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IContainerRegistry
member.summary: Represents container registry information for deployment targets.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IContainerRegistry.Name
member.summary: Gets the name of the container registry.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IContainerRegistry.Endpoint
member.summary: Gets the endpoint URL of the container registry.
member.remarks: An empty endpoint value indicates a local container registry where images are built and used locally without being pushed to a remote registry (e.g., Docker Compose scenarios).

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IContainerRegistry.Repository
member.summary: Gets the repository path within the container registry.
member.remarks:
The repository represents the namespace or path segment that appears after the registry endpoint in a container image reference. For example:
For Docker Hub (docker.io): typically a username like captainsafia
For GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io): typically username/reponame
For Azure Container Registry: typically left empty as images are pushed directly to the registry
When not , the repository is combined with the image name to form the full image path: {endpoint}/{repository}/{imageName}:{tag}. When , the image path is: {endpoint}/{imageName}:{tag}.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.NameValidationPolicyAnnotation
member.summary: Represents an annotation that customizes the name validation rules applied to a resource when it is added to the application model.
member.remarks: By default, resource names must be 1–64 ASCII characters long, start with a letter, contain only letters, digits, and hyphens, and not contain consecutive or trailing hyphens. Use this annotation to relax individual rules. The policy disables every rule, which is useful for internal resources (such as installers or rebuilders) that append suffixes to user-provided resource names and are never deployed.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.NameValidationPolicyAnnotation.Default
member.summary: The default policy that enforces all standard name validation rules.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.NameValidationPolicyAnnotation.None
member.summary: A policy that disables all name validation rules.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.NameValidationPolicyAnnotation.MaxLength
member.summary: Gets the maximum allowed length for the resource name, or to disable length validation. Defaults to .

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.NameValidationPolicyAnnotation.ValidateStartsWithLetter
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether to validate that the name starts with an ASCII letter. Defaults to .

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.NameValidationPolicyAnnotation.ValidateAllowedCharacters
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether to validate that the name contains only ASCII letters, digits, and hyphens. Defaults to .

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.NameValidationPolicyAnnotation.ValidateNoConsecutiveHyphens
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether to validate that the name does not contain consecutive hyphens. Defaults to .

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.NameValidationPolicyAnnotation.ValidateNoTrailingHyphen
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether to validate that the name does not end with a hyphen. Defaults to .

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.KnownHostNames.Localhost
member.summary: The host name associated with the IP loopback interface (localhost).
member.remarks: In general, "localhost" resolves to multiple addresses. E.g. in dual-stack systems (IPv4 and IPv6, very common) "localhost" resolves at least to 127.0.0.1 and [::1]. On some systems there are multiple IPv4 networks associated with loopback interface and the number of potential addresses for "localhost" increases accordingly.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.GenerateParameterDefault
member.summary: Represents that a default value should be generated.
member.remarks:
The recommended minimum bits of entropy for a generated password is 128 bits.
The general calculation of bits of entropy is:
log base 2 (numberPossibleOutputs)
This generator uses 23 upper case, 23 lower case (excludes i,l,o,I,L,O to prevent confusion), 10 numeric, and 11 special characters. So a total of 67 possible characters.
When all character sets are enabled, the number of possible outputs is (67 ^ length). The minimum password length for 128 bits of entropy is 22 characters: log base 2 (67 ^ 22).
When character sets are disabled, it lowers the number of possible outputs and thus the bits of entropy.
Using MinLower, MinUpper, MinNumeric, and MinSpecial also lowers the number of possible outputs and thus the bits of entropy.
A generalized lower-bound formula for the number of possible outputs is to consider a string of the form:
{nonRequiredCharacters}{requiredCharacters}
let a = MinLower, b = MinUpper, c = MinNumeric, d = MinSpecial let x = length - (a + b + c + d)
nonRequiredPossibilities = 67^x requiredPossibilities = 23^a * 23^b * 10^c * 11^d * (a + b + c + d)! / (a! * b! * c! * d!)
lower-bound of total possibilities = nonRequiredPossibilities * requiredPossibilities
Putting it all together, the lower-bound bits of entropy calculation is:
log base 2 [67^x * 23^a * 23^b * 10^c * 11^d * (a + b + c + d)! / (a! * b! * c! * d!)]

member.summary: Represents an expression that might be made up of multiple resource properties. For example, a connection string might be made up of a host, port, and password from different endpoints.

remarks.para: A operates in one of two modes:

list.item: Value mode — a format string with interpolated parameters (e.g., "redis://{0}:{1}").
Conditional mode — a ternary-style expression that selects between two branch expressions based on the string value of a . Created via .

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ResourceCommandService.ExecuteCommandAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Execute a command for the specified resource.

remarks.para: A resource id can be either the unique id of the resource or the displayed resource name.

Projects, executables and containers typically have a unique id that combines the display name and a unique suffix. For example, a resource named cache could have a resource id of cache-abcdwxyz. This id is used to uniquely identify the resource in the app host.

The resource name can be also be used to retrieve the resource state, but it must be unique. If there are multiple resources with the same name, then this method will not return a match. For example, if a resource named cache has multiple replicas, then specifing cache won't return a match.

member.param: The resource id. This id can either exactly match the unique id of the resource or the displayed resource name if the resource name doesn't have duplicates (i.e. replicas).
The command name.
The cancellation token.
member.returns: The indicates command success or failure.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ResourceCommandService.ExecuteCommandAsync(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResource,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Execute a command for the specified resource.
member.param: The resource. If the resource has multiple instances, such as replicas, then the command will be executed for each instance.
The command name.
The cancellation token.
member.returns: The indicates command success or failure.

member.summary: Discover the full transitive closure of all dependencies. This includes direct dependencies and all dependencies of those dependencies, recursively.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ResourceDependencyDiscoveryMode.DirectOnly
member.summary: Discover only direct dependencies. This includes dependencies from annotations (parent, wait, connection string redirect) and from environment variables and command-line arguments, but does not recurse into the dependencies of those dependencies.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ResourceNotificationService.TryGetCurrentState(System.String,Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ResourceEvent@)
member.summary: Attempts to retrieve the current state of a resource by resourceId.

remarks.para: A resource id can be either the unique id of the resource or the displayed resource name.

Projects, executables and containers typically have a unique id that combines the display name and a unique suffix. For example, a resource named cache could have a resource id of cache-abcdwxyz. This id is used to uniquely identify the resource in the app host.

The resource name can be also be used to retrieve the resource state, but it must be unique. If there are multiple resources with the same name, then this method will not return a match. For example, if a resource named cache has multiple replicas, then specifing cache won't return a match.

member.param: The resource id. This id can either exactly match the unique id of the resource or the displayed resource name if the resource name doesn't have duplicates (i.e. replicas).
When this method returns, contains the for the specified resource id, if found; otherwise, .
member.returns: if specified resource id was found; otherwise, .

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ValueSnapshot`1
member.summary:
Provides an asynchronously initialized value that: - Can be awaited via GetValueAsync() until the first value or exception is set. - Exposes the latest value after it has been set (supports re-setting). - Tracks whether a value or exception was ever set via IsValueSet. - Supports setting an exception that will be thrown by GetValueAsync.
Thread-safe for concurrent SetValue / SetException / GetValueAsync calls.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ValueSnapshot`1.IsValueSet
member.summary: True once a value or exception has been set at least once.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ValueSnapshot`1.GetValueAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Await the current value: - If a value has already been set, returns it immediately. - If an exception has been set, throws it. - Otherwise waits until the first value or exception is set. Always returns the latest value at the moment of completion or throws the exception.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ValueSnapshot`1.SetValue(`0)
member.summary: Sets (or updates) the value. The first successful call completes any pending GetValueAsync waiters. Subsequent calls replace the current value.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ValueSnapshot`1.SetException(System.Exception)
member.summary: Sets an exception that will be thrown by GetValueAsync. The first successful call (either SetValue or SetException) completes any pending GetValueAsync waiters.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EnvironmentVariableNameEncoder.ValidNameRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$
Options:
RegexOptions.CultureInvariant
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match a character in the set [A-Z_a-z].
○ Match a character in the set [0-9A-Z_a-z] atomically any number of times.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.summary: Returns an environment-variable-safe representation of the provided name.
member.param: The raw name.
member.returns: A string that is safe to use as part of an environment variable.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Ats.CollectionExports
member.summary: ATS intrinsic collection capabilities for Dict and List operations.

remarks.para: These capabilities provide first-class support for mutable collections in polyglot app hosts. Guest languages wrap these in idiomatic collection classes (e.g., AtsDict, AtsList in TypeScript).

item.description: Mutable collections (Dictionary, List) return handles to the collection
Immutable collections (IReadOnlyDictionary, IReadOnlyList, arrays) return serialized copies
All operations are async (round-trip to host)

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Ats.CoreExports
member.summary: Core ATS (Aspire Type System) exports for polyglot app host support.

remarks.para: This class defines the foundational capabilities that enable non-.NET languages (TypeScript, Python, etc.) to build Aspire distributed applications. These exports form the stable API surface for polyglot app hosts.

item.description: Capabilities are the contract - not CLR method signatures
Handles replace direct object references - guest code never sees .NET types
.NET implementation details are hidden behind a stable polyglot surface

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Ats.CoreExports.WithVolume(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ContainerResource},System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds a volume to a container resource.

remarks.para: Volumes persist data across container restarts. Named volumes are managed by Docker/Podman and stored in a system-managed location.

Why this wrapper exists: The original ContainerResourceBuilderExtensions.WithVolume has parameter order (name?, target, isReadOnly) where the optional name comes first. This wrapper reorders parameters to (target, name?, isReadOnly) so the required target parameter comes first, providing a better API for polyglot consumers.

member.param: The container resource builder handle.
The mount path inside the container.
The volume name. If null, an anonymous volume is created.
Whether the volume is read-only.
member.returns: The same resource builder handle for chaining.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Ats.CoreExports.GetResourceName(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResource})
member.summary: Gets the name of the resource from a builder.
member.remarks: Why this wrapper exists: This capability accesses a nested property (resource.Resource.Name) which requires a wrapper method. There is no single .NET method that returns just the resource name that could be annotated directly.
member.param: The resource builder handle.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.AspireDtoAttribute
member.summary: Marks a class as an ATS (Aspire Type System) Data Transfer Object. DTOs are serializable types used to pass structured data to capabilities.

remarks.para: DTOs should be simple record or class types with properties that can be serialized to/from JSON. They should not contain complex .NET types like IConfiguration, ILogger, or other framework-specific types.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.AspireDtoAttribute.DtoTypeId
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional type identifier for this DTO. If not specified, the DTO will be serialized as a plain JSON object.
member.remarks: When set, the serialized JSON will include a $type field with this value.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.AspireExportAttribute
member.summary: Marks a method, type, or assembly-level type as an ATS (Aspire Type System) export.

remarks.para: This attribute serves multiple purposes:

item.description: Capability exports (on methods): Marks a static method as an ATS capability. The capability ID is automatically derived as {AssemblyName}/{camelCaseMethodName}. For example: AddRedis in Aspire.Hosting.Redis becomes Aspire.Hosting.Redis/addRedis. Specify an explicit id only when disambiguation is needed (e.g., multiple overloads).

Type exports (on types): Marks a type as an ATS-exported type. The type ID is automatically derived as {AssemblyName}/{TypeName}. For example: RedisResource in Aspire.Hosting.Redis becomes Aspire.Hosting.Redis/RedisResource.
Context types (on types with ExposeProperties): When is true, the type's properties are automatically exposed as get/set capabilities for use in callbacks.
External type exports (assembly-level): For types you don't own, use assembly-level with property to include them in the ATS type system.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.AspireExportAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance for a capability export (on methods) with an explicit capability ID.
member.param: The capability name for this method. The full capability ID is computed as {AssemblyName}/{id}. Use this overload only when disambiguation is needed (e.g., multiple overloads of the same method). When not specified, the capability ID is automatically derived from the method name using camelCase.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.AspireExportAttribute.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance for a type or method export.
member.remarks: For type exports, the type ID is automatically derived as {AssemblyName}/{TypeName}. Set to true for context types whose properties should be exposed as get/set capabilities. For method exports, the capability ID is automatically derived from the method name using camelCase.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.AspireExportAttribute.#ctor(System.Type)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance for an assembly-level type export.
member.param: The CLR type to export to ATS.
member.remarks: Use this constructor at assembly level for types you don't own. The type ID is derived as {type.Assembly.Name}/{type.Name}.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.AspireExportAttribute.Id
member.summary: Gets the method name for capability exports.

remarks.para: The full capability ID is computed as {AssemblyName}/{Id}.
This is null for type exports.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.AspireExportAttribute.Type
member.summary: Gets or sets the CLR type for assembly-level type exports.
member.remarks: Use this at assembly level to export types you don't own to ATS.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.AspireExportAttribute.Description
member.summary: Gets or sets a description of what this export does.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.AspireExportAttribute.MethodName
member.summary: Gets or sets the method name to use in generated polyglot SDKs.

remarks.para: When not specified, the method name from is used directly.

Use this property to override the generated name when disambiguation is needed (e.g., to avoid collisions with another integration's method of the same name). Each language generator will apply its own formatting convention (camelCase for TypeScript, snake_case for Python, etc.).

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.AspireExportAttribute.ExposeProperties
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to expose properties of this type as ATS capabilities.

remarks.para: When true, the type's public instance properties that return ATS-compatible types are automatically exposed as get/set capabilities (unless marked with ).
Use this for context types passed to callbacks (like EnvironmentCallbackContext) that provide access to runtime state.

Property capabilities are named as:
{Package}/{TypeName}.{propertyName} for getters (camelCase property name)
{Package}/{TypeName}.set{PropertyName} for setters (writable properties only)

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.AspireExportAttribute.ExposeMethods
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to expose public instance methods of this type as ATS capabilities.

remarks.para: When true, the type's public instance methods are automatically exposed as capabilities (unless marked with ).
Method capabilities are named as {Package}/{TypeName}.{methodName} (camelCase method name). The first parameter of the capability will be a handle to the instance.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.AspireExportAttribute.RunSyncOnBackgroundThread
member.summary: Gets or sets whether synchronous exported methods should be invoked on a background thread by the ATS dispatcher.

remarks.para: Set this to for synchronous exports that may invoke synchronous callback delegates which in turn re-enter the remote host through ATS. Running the export on a background thread allows the JSON-RPC request loop to continue processing nested callback and capability invocations while the synchronous method waits for the callback to complete.
This setting only affects synchronous exported methods. Async exports that already return or are awaited normally and do not use this option.
When applied to a type, this setting also applies to exported members discovered from that type unless an individual member overrides it.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.AspireExportIgnoreAttribute
member.summary: Excludes a property, method, or type from ATS export when the containing type uses or .

remarks.para: Use this attribute on individual members to opt them out of automatic exposure when the containing type uses ExposeProperties = true or ExposeMethods = true.
This is useful when most members should be exposed but a few contain internal implementation details or types that shouldn't be part of the polyglot API.
Apply this attribute to a type to suppress all automatic export coverage checks for the type's members when the type is intentionally not part of the ATS surface.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.AspireExportIgnoreAttribute.Reason
member.summary: Gets or sets the reason why this member is excluded from ATS export.
member.remarks: Use this property to document why an API cannot be exported to polyglot app hosts, distinguishing reviewed-but-incompatible members from those not yet reviewed.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.AspireUnionAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that a parameter or property accepts a union of multiple ATS types.

remarks.para: Use on method parameters to indicate the parameter accepts multiple types:
remarks.code: [AspireExport("withEnvironment")] public static IResourceBuilder<T> WithEnvironment<T>( this IResourceBuilder<T> builder, string name, [AspireUnion(typeof(string), typeof(ReferenceExpression))] object value)
remarks.para: Use on properties (especially Dictionary<string, object>) to specify the value type union:
Important: All types in the union must be valid ATS types (primitives, handles, DTOs, or collections thereof). The scanner will fail with an error if any type cannot be mapped to ATS.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.AspireUnionAttribute.#ctor(System.Type[])
member.summary: Initializes a new instance with the types that form the union.
member.param: The CLR types that form the union. Must have at least 2 types.
member.exception: Thrown when is null.
Thrown when has fewer than 2 types.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.AspireUnionAttribute.Types
member.summary: Gets the CLR types that form the union.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.AspireValueAttribute
member.summary: Marks an immutable static field or property as an ATS-exported value.

remarks.para: Exported values are snapped during ATS scanning and emitted into generated guest SDKs as predefined values. They are intended for immutable DTO, enum, and primitive values that should be available without re-declaring them in each guest language.
The becomes the root name of the generated value catalog in guest SDKs. Nested static types are emitted as nested namespaces or classes under that root.
Exported values should be side-effect free and should not return handles or other runtime-bound ATS types.

member.param: The root name of the generated value catalog.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.AspireValueAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Marks an immutable static field or property as an ATS-exported value.

remarks.para: Exported values are snapped during ATS scanning and emitted into generated guest SDKs as predefined values. They are intended for immutable DTO, enum, and primitive values that should be available without re-declaring them in each guest language.
The becomes the root name of the generated value catalog in guest SDKs. Nested static types are emitted as nested namespaces or classes under that root.
Exported values should be side-effect free and should not return handles or other runtime-bound ATS types.

member.param: The root name of the generated value catalog.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.AspireValueAttribute.CatalogName
member.summary: Gets the root name of the generated value catalog.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.AspireValueAttribute.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional override for the exported value name.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants
member.summary: Shared constants and helpers for backchannel socket communication between AppHost and CLI. These MUST stay in sync between both components.

remarks.para: The backchannel is a Unix domain socket that enables bidirectional communication:

list.item: CLI → AppHost: Commands (stop, get info, etc.)

remarks.para: Socket files are stored in: ~/.aspire/cli/backchannels/
New format: auxi.sock.{appHostHash}.{instanceHash}.{pid}

list.item: auxi.sock - Prefix (not "aux" because that's reserved on Windows)
{appHostHash} - xxHash(AppHost project path)[0:16] - identifies the AppHost project
{instanceHash} - random hex identifier[0:12] - makes each socket name non-deterministic
{pid} - Process ID of the AppHost - identifies the specific instance

remarks.para: Previous format (for backward compatibility): auxi.sock.{appHostHash}.{pid}
Old format (for backward compatibility): auxi.sock.{appHostHash}

list.item: Multiple instances of the same AppHost can run simultaneously
Orphan detection: if PID doesn't exist, socket is orphaned and can be deleted
Fast cleanup without needing to attempt connection

remarks.para: Old CLI versions use glob pattern aux*.sock.* which matches the new format. Old CLIs will work with new AppHosts, they just won't benefit from PID-based orphan detection.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.SocketPrefix
member.summary: Prefix for auxiliary backchannel sockets.
member.remarks: Uses "auxi" instead of "aux" because "aux" is a reserved device name on Windows (from DOS days: CON, PRN, AUX, NUL, COM1-9, LPT1-9). Using "aux" causes "SocketException: A socket operation encountered a dead network" on Windows.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.HashLength
member.summary: Number of hex characters to use from the stable xxHash-based AppHost identifier.
member.remarks: Using 16 chars (64 bits) balances uniqueness against path length constraints. Unix socket paths are limited to ~104 characters on most systems. Full path example: ~/.aspire/cli/backchannels/auxi.sock.bc43b855b6848166.a1b2c3d4e5f6.46730 = ~78 characters, well under the limit.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.CompactIdentifierLength
member.summary: Number of hex characters to use for compact local identifiers.
member.remarks: Using 12 chars (48 bits) keeps socket and package cache paths short while still providing enough variation for local file names that are not part of a security boundary.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.InstanceHashLength
member.summary: Number of hex characters to use from the randomized instance identifier.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.GetBackchannelsDirectory(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the backchannels directory path for the given home directory.
member.param: The user's home directory.
member.returns: The full path to the backchannels directory.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.ComputeHash(System.String)
member.summary: Computes the hash portion of the socket name from an AppHost path.
member.remarks: On Windows the drive letter is normalized to uppercase before hashing so that "C:\App\MyApp.csproj" and "c:\App\MyApp.csproj" produce the same hash. Without this, the CLI and AppHost can derive different drive-letter casings (e.g. FileInfo.FullName vs MSBuild metadata) and fail to match sockets.
member.param: The full path to the AppHost project file.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.ComputeLegacyHash(System.String)
member.summary: Computes the legacy (pre-normalization) hash for backward compatibility.
member.remarks: Older AppHost versions hashed the raw path without case normalization. The CLI uses this to find sockets created by older AppHosts. Returns null when the legacy hash would be identical to .
member.param: The full path to the AppHost project file.
member.returns: A 16-character lowercase hex string, or null if it matches the current hash.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.NormalizePath(System.String)
member.summary: Normalizes the path for consistent hashing by uppercasing the Windows drive letter.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.ComputeSocketPath(System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Computes the full socket path for an AppHost instance.
member.remarks: Called by AppHost when creating the socket. Includes a randomized instance hash and the PID to ensure uniqueness across multiple instances of the same AppHost.
member.param: The full path to the AppHost project file.
The user's home directory.
The process ID of the AppHost.
member.returns: The full socket path including PID.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.ComputeSocketPrefix(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Computes the socket path prefix for finding sockets (without PID).
member.remarks: Called by CLI when searching for sockets. Since the CLI doesn't know the AppHost's PID, it uses this prefix with a glob pattern to find matching sockets.
member.param: The full path to the AppHost project file.
The user's home directory.
member.returns: The socket path prefix (without PID suffix).

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.FindMatchingSockets(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Finds all socket files matching the given AppHost path.
member.remarks: Returns all socket files for an AppHost, regardless of PID. This includes old format (auxi.sock.{hash}), previous format (auxi.sock.{hash}.{pid}), and current format (auxi.sock.{hash}.{instanceHash}.{pid}).
member.param: The full path to the AppHost project file.
The user's home directory.
member.returns: An array of socket file paths, or empty if none found.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.FindSocketsByPrefix(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Finds socket files in whose names match in any of the supported socket name formats.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.ExtractHash(System.String)
member.summary: Extracts the hash from a socket filename.
member.remarks: Works with old format (auxi.sock.{hash}), previous format (auxi.sock.{hash}.{pid}), and current format (auxi.sock.{hash}.{instanceHash}.{pid}).
member.param: The full socket path or filename.
member.returns: The hash portion, or null if the format is unrecognized.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.ExtractPid(System.String)
member.summary: Extracts the PID from a socket filename when one is present.
member.param: The full socket path or filename.
member.returns: The PID if present and valid, or null for old format sockets.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.ProcessExists(System.Int32)
member.summary: Checks if a process with the given PID exists and is running.
member.remarks: Used for orphan detection. If the PID from a socket filename doesn't correspond to a running process, the socket is orphaned and can be safely deleted.
member.param: The process ID to check.
member.returns: true if the process exists and is running; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.CleanupOrphanedSockets(System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Cleans up orphaned socket files for a specific AppHost hash.

remarks.para: Called by AppHost on startup to clean up sockets from previous crashed instances. This ensures orphan cleanup happens even if the user has an old CLI that doesn't support PID-based orphan detection.

Limitation: This method only cleans up sockets that include a PID (auxi.sock.{hash}.{pid} or auxi.sock.{hash}.{instanceHash}.{pid}) because old format sockets (auxi.sock.{hash}) don't have a PID for orphan detection. Old format sockets are cleaned up via connection-based detection in the CLI.

member.param: The backchannels directory path.
The AppHost hash to match.
The current process ID (to avoid deleting own socket).
member.returns: The number of orphaned sockets deleted.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.ComputeStableIdentifier(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Computes a compact stable identifier from a string value.
member.remarks: Uses XxHash3 because these identifiers are only used for local naming and lookup. They do not protect secrets or cross a trust boundary, so a fast non-cryptographic hash is preferable to SHA-2.
member.param: The string value to hash.
The number of lowercase hex characters to return.
member.returns: A lowercase hex identifier truncated to characters.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Backchannel.BackchannelConstants.CreateRandomIdentifier(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a compact randomized identifier.
member.param: The number of lowercase hex characters to return.
member.returns: A lowercase hex identifier truncated to characters.

member.summary: Provides extension methods for adding container registry resources to the distributed application.

The name of the container registry resource.
An containing the registry endpoint URL or hostname.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ContainerRegistryResourceBuilderExtensions.AddContainerRegistry(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a container registry resource to the application model with literal string values.
member.param: The distributed application builder.
The name of the container registry resource.
The registry endpoint URL or hostname (e.g., "docker.io", "ghcr.io").
The optional repository path within the registry (e.g., "myusername" for Docker Hub, "owner/repo" for GHCR).
member.returns: An for the container registry resource.
member.example:
Add a Docker Hub container registry:
var registry = builder.AddContainerRegistry("docker-hub", "docker.io", "myusername");

Add a GitHub Container Registry:
var registry = builder.AddContainerRegistry("ghcr", "ghcr.io", "owner/repo");

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ContainerResourceBuilderExtensions.WithVolume``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds a volume to a container resource.
member.param: The resource builder.
The name of the volume.
The target path where the volume is mounted in the container.
A flag that indicates if the volume should be mounted as read-only.

remarks.para: Volumes are used to persist file-based data generated by and used by the container. They are managed by the container runtime and can be shared among multiple containers. They are not shared with the host's file-system. To mount files from the host inside the container, call .

If a value for the of the volume is not provided, the volume is created as an "anonymous volume" and will be given a random name by the container runtime. To share a volume between multiple containers, specify the same .
The path specifies the path the volume will be mounted inside the container's file system.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ContainerResourceBuilderExtensions.WithVolume``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String)

The target path where the volume is mounted in the container.

remarks.para: Volumes are used to persist file-based data generated by and used by the container. They are managed by the container runtime and can be shared among multiple containers. They are not shared with the host's file-system. To mount files from the host inside the container, call .
This overload will create an "anonymous volume" and will be given a random name by the container runtime. To share a volume between multiple containers, call and specify the same value for name.
The path specifies the path the volume will be mounted inside the container's file system.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ContainerResourceBuilderExtensions.WithBindMount``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds a bind mount to a container resource.
member.param: The resource builder.
The source path of the mount. This is the path to the file or directory on the host, relative to the app host project directory.
The target path where the file or directory is mounted in the container.
A flag that indicates if this is a read-only mount.

remarks.para: Bind mounts are used to mount files or directories from the host file-system into the container. If the host doesn't require access to the files, consider using volumes instead via .

The path specifies the path of the file or directory on the host that will be mounted in the container. If the path is not absolute, it will be evaluated relative to the app host project directory path.
The path specifies the path the file or directory will be mounted inside the container's file system.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ContainerResourceBuilderExtensions.WithDockerfile``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Causes Aspire to build the specified container image from a Dockerfile.
member.typeparam: Type parameter specifying any type derived from /
member.param: The .
Path to be used as the context for the container image build.
Path to the Dockerfile relative to the . Defaults to "Dockerfile" if not specified.
The stage representing the image to be published in a multi-stage Dockerfile.

remarks.para: When this method is called an annotation is added to the that specifies the context path and Dockerfile path to be used when building the container image. These details are then used by the orchestrator to build the image before using that image to start the container.
The is relative to the AppHost directory unless it is a fully qualified path. The is relative to the unless it is a fully qualified path. If the is not provided, it defaults to "Dockerfile" in the .

When generating the manifest for deployment tools, the method results in an additional attribute being added to the `container.v0` resource type which contains the configuration necessary to allow the deployment tool to build the container image prior to deployment.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ContainerResourceBuilderExtensions.WithDockerfileFactory``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String,System.Func{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.DockerfileFactoryContext,System.String},System.String)
member.summary: Builds the specified container image from a Dockerfile generated by a synchronous factory function.
member.typeparam: Type parameter specifying any type derived from .
member.param: The .
Path to be used as the context for the container image build.
A synchronous function that returns the Dockerfile content as a string.
The stage representing the image to be published in a multi-stage Dockerfile.

remarks.para: When this method is called, an annotation is added to the that specifies the context path and a factory function that generates Dockerfile content. The factory is invoked at build time to produce the Dockerfile, which is then written to a temporary file and used by the orchestrator to build the container image.
The is relative to the AppHost directory unless it is fully qualified.
The factory function is invoked once during the build process to generate the Dockerfile content. The output is trusted and not validated.

member.remarks: This method is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use instead.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ContainerResourceBuilderExtensions.WithContainerName``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String)
member.summary: Overrides the default container name for this resource. By default Aspire generates a unique container name based on the resource name and a random postfix (or a postfix based on a hash of the AppHost project path for persistent container resources). This method allows you to override that behavior with a custom name, but could lead to naming conflicts if the specified name is not unique.
member.remarks: Combining this with will allow Aspire to re-use an existing container that was not created by an Aspire AppHost.
member.param: The resource builder for the container resource.
The desired container name. Must be a valid container name or your runtime will report an error.

member.summary: Creates or updates files and/or folders at the destination path in the container.
member.param: The resource builder for the container resource.
The destination (absolute) path in the container.
The file system entries to create.
The default owner UID for the created or updated file system. Defaults to 0 for root if not set.
The default group ID for the created or updated file system. Defaults to 0 for root if not set.
The umask permissions to exclude from the default file and folder permissions. This takes away (rather than granting) default permissions to files and folders without an explicit mode permission set.

remarks.para: For containers with a lifetime, changing the contents of create file entries will result in the container being recreated. Make sure any data being written to containers is idempotent for a given app model configuration. Specifically, be careful not to include any data that will be unique on a per-run basis.

member.remarks: This method is not available in polyglot app hosts.

member.summary: Creates or updates files and/or folders at the destination path in the container by copying them from a source path on the host. In run mode, this will copy the files from the host to the container at runtime, allowing for overriding ownership and permissions in the container. In publish mode, this will create a bind mount to the source path on the host.
member.param: The resource builder for the container resource.
The destination (absolute) path in the container.
The source path on the host to copy files from.
The default owner UID for the created or updated file system. Defaults to 0 for root if not set.

The umask permissions to exclude from the default file and folder permissions. This takes away (rather than granting) default permissions to files and folders without an explicit mode permission set.
member.remarks: This method is not available in polyglot app hosts.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ContainerResourceBuilderExtensions.WithEndpointProxySupport``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Set whether a container resource can use proxied endpoints or whether they should be disabled for all endpoints belonging to the container. If set to false, endpoints belonging to the container resource will ignore the configured proxy settings and run proxy-less.
member.param: The resource builder for the container resource.
Should endpoints for the container resource support using a proxy?
member.remarks: This method is intended to support scenarios with persistent lifetime containers where it is desirable for the container to be accessible over the same port whether the Aspire application is running or not. Proxied endpoints bind ports that are only accessible while the Aspire application is running. The user needs to be careful to ensure that container endpoints are using unique ports when disabling proxy support as by default for proxy-less endpoints, Aspire will allocate the internal container port as the host port, which will increase the chance of port conflicts.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Dashboard.DashboardService
member.summary: Implements a gRPC service that a dashboard can consume.

member.summary: Implements a gRPC service that a dashboard can consume.
member.remarks: An instance of this type is created for every gRPC service call, so it may not hold onto any state required beyond a single request. Longer-scoped data is stored in .

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Dashboard.DashboardService.ApplicationNameRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^(?<name>.+?)\\.?AppHost$
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase | RegexOptions.ExplicitCapture | RegexOptions.Singleline | RegexOptions.CultureInvariant
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ "name" capture group.
○ Match any character lazily at least once.
○ Match '.' atomically, optionally.
○ Match a character in the set [Aa].
○ Match a character in the set [Pp] exactly 2 times.
○ Match a character in the set [Hh].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Ss].
○ Match a character in the set [Tt].
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.summary: Examines the Aspire resource annotations and adds equivalent ServiceProducerAnnotations to the corresponding DCP resource.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Dcp.DcpModelUtilities.NormalizeTargetHost(System.String)
member.summary: Normalize the target host to a tuple of (address, binding mode) to a single valid address for service discovery purposes. A user may have configured an endpoint target host that isn't itself a valid IP address or hostname that can be resolved by other services or clients. For example, 0.0.0.0 is considered to mean that the service should bind to all IPv4 addresses. When the target host indicates that the service should bind to all IPv4 or IPv6 addresses, we instead return "localhost" as the address as that is a valid address for the .NET dev certificate. The binding mode is metadata that indicates whether an endpoint is bound to a single address or some set of multiple addresses on the system.
member.param: The target host from an EndpointAnnotation

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Dcp.DcpOptions.ContainerRuntimeInitializationTimeout
member.summary: The duration to wait for the container runtime to become healthy before aborting startup.
member.remarks: A value of zero, which is the default value, indicates that the application will not wait for the container runtime to become healthy. If this property has a value greater than zero, the application will abort startup if the container runtime does not become healthy within the specified timeout.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Dcp.DcpOptions.WaitForResourceCleanup
member.summary: Whether to wait for resource cleanup to end when stopping DcpExecutor. This guarantees that application resources (programs, transient containers etc.) are stopped before DcpExecutor.StopAsync() returns. Default is false (resources are cleaned up asynchronously).

member.summary: Returns a log stream for the specified resource.

The cancellation token for the stream retrieval operation (does not affect the returned stream).
If true, the log stream will be followed until the resource is deleted or the stream is disposed of.
If true, timestamps (RFC3339) will be included in the log stream.
If true, line numbers will be included in the log stream.
If specified, limits the number of log linets returned. Cannot be used with "follow".

If specified, limits the response to at most N existing, NEWEST log lines. If "follow" is true, new log lines that appear after the log stream was created do not count against the limit, and will be streamed until the client closes the stream.
If specified, skips the first N log lines in the result set. Cannot be used together with "tail".

member.summary: Finished means the Executable ran to completion.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.Dcp.Model.ExecutableState.Unknown
member.summary: Unknown means we are not tracking the actual-state counterpart of the Executable (process or IDE run session). As a result, we do not know whether it already finished, and what is the exit code, if any. This can happen if a controller launches a process and then terminates. When a new controller instance comes online, it may see non-zero ExecutionID Status, but it does not track the corresponding process or IDE session.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Utils.ContainerReferenceParser
member.summary: Class to parse container references (e.g. "mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:8.0")

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Utils.ContainerReferenceParser.ImageNameRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^((?<registry>((localhost|\\[(?:[a-fA-F0-9:]+)\\]|([\\w-]+(\\.[\\w-]+)+))(:\\d+)?)|([\\w-]+:\\d+))\\/)?(?<image>[\\w-./<>]+)(:(?<tag>[\\w-.<>]+))?(@(?<digest>.+))?$
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ 1st capture group.
○ "registry" capture group.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ 2nd capture group.
○ 3rd capture group.
○ Match with 3 alternative expressions.
○ Match the string "localhost".
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match '['.
○ Match a character in the set [0-:A-Fa-f] atomically at least once.
○ Match ']'.
○ 4th capture group.
○ Match a character in the set [-\w] atomically at least once.
○ Loop greedily at least once.
○ 5th capture group.
○ Match '.'.
○ Match a character in the set [-\w] greedily at least once.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ 6th capture group.
○ Match ':'.
○ Match a Unicode digit atomically at least once.
○ 7th capture group.
○ Match a character in the set [-\w] atomically at least once.
○ Match ':'.
○ Match a Unicode digit atomically at least once.
○ Match '/'.
○ "image" capture group.
○ Match a character in the set [--/<>\w] greedily at least once.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ 8th capture group.
○ Match ':'.
○ "tag" capture group.
○ Match a character in the set [-.<>\w] greedily at least once.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ 9th capture group.
○ Match '@'.
○ "digest" capture group.
○ Match a character other than '\n' greedily at least once.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.
