member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Utils.PeriodicRestartAsyncEnumerable.CreateAsync``1(System.Func{System.Nullable{``0},System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.Task{System.Collections.Generic.IAsyncEnumerable{``0}}},System.TimeSpan,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Creates an async enumerable that wraps and periodically restarts an inner async enumeration. This is intended to keep long running watch enumerations fresh and will recreate the watch enumeration on a set interval or if the inner enumerable terminates unexpectedly. The goal is to ensure that we keep the wrapped enumeration active until the main token is cancelled.
member.typeparam: The type the enumerable iterates over
member.param: Factory method that takes the last iterrated value (if one exists) and a and returns a fresh to enumerate over
How often should we get a new enumerable from the factory
Stop all enumeration once this is cancelled
member.returns: An of items returned by the inner iterables

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Utils.PathNormalizer.NormalizePathForStorage(System.String)
member.summary: Normalizes a path for storage in configuration files by replacing backslash separators with forward slashes.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Utils.PathNormalizer.ResolveToFilesystemPath(System.String)
member.summary: On Windows, resolves a path to its filesystem-canonical form by querying the OS for the actual casing of each path component. On other platforms this is a no-op because the file system is case-sensitive and there is no casing ambiguity.
member.remarks: Use this when the path needs to match what MSBuild reports for the same file. MSBuild always uses the true filesystem casing; a user who types --apphost c:\FOO\bar.csproj will get back C:\foo\bar.csproj if that is the on-disk casing, making the hash agree with the AppHost side.
member.param: An absolute path to a file that exists on disk.
member.returns: The path with OS-canonical casing, or unchanged if it cannot be resolved (file does not exist, UNC path, etc.).

member.summary: Returns the certificate PEM format key and/or PFX bytes for the specified certificate. On macOS, both outputs are cached as separate files to avoid triggering repeated keychain prompts. The cache is read without loading any PFX into an X509Certificate2, because EphemeralKeySet is not supported on macOS with net8.0 and loading without it imports the private key into the keychain.
member.param: The certificate to export key material from.
The password for the private key, or null for unencrypted export.
Whether to export the private key in PEM format.
Whether to export the certificate in PFX format.
A token that can be used to cancel the operation.
member.returns: A tuple containing the PEM-encoded key and PFX bytes.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplication
member.summary: Represents a distributed application that implements the and interfaces.

remarks.para: The is an implementation of the interface that orchestrates an Aspire application. To build an instance of the class, use the method to create an instance of the interface. Using the interface you can configure the resources that comprise the distributed application and describe the dependencies between them.
Once the distributed application has been defined use the method to create an instance of the class. The class exposes a method which then starts the distributed application and its resources.

The method provides additional options for constructing the including disabling the Aspire dashboard (see ) or allowing unsecured communication between the browser and dashboard, and dashboard and app host (see .

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplication.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.IHost)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The instance.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplication.CreateBuilder
member.summary: Creates a new instance of the interface.
member.returns: A new instance of the interface.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplication.CreateBuilder(System.String[])
member.summary: Creates a new instance of with the specified command-line arguments.
member.param: The command-line arguments to use when building the distributed application.

remarks.para: The method is the most common way to create an instance of the interface. Typically this method will be called as a top-level statement in the application's entry-point.

Note that the parameter is a and is essential in allowing the application host to work with deployment tools because arguments are used to tell the application host that it is in publish mode. If is not provided the application will not work with deployment tools. It is also possible to provide arguments using the overload of this method.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplication.CreateBuilder(Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplicationOptions)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of the interface with the specified .
member.param: The to use for configuring the builder.
member.returns: A new instance of the interface.

remarks.para: The method provides greater control over the behavior of the distributed application at runtime. For example providing an argument allows developers to force all container images to be loaded from a specified container registry by using the property, or disabling the dashboard by using the property. Refer to the class for more details on each option that may be provided.
When supplying a custom it is recommended to populate the property to ensure that the app host continues to function correctly when used with deployment tools that need to enable publish mode.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplication.CreateBuilder(Aspire.Hosting.Ats.CreateBuilderOptions)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of the interface with the specified options. This overload is designed for polyglot apphosts (TypeScript, Python, etc.) and exposes a simplified options DTO.
member.param: The to use for configuring the builder.
member.returns: A new instance of the interface.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplication.Services
member.summary: Gets the instance configured for the application.

remarks.para: The is an implementation and as such exposes a property which allows developers to get services from the dependency injection container after instance has been built using the method.
To add services to the dependency injection container developers should use the property to access the instance.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplication.ResourceNotifications
member.summary: Gets the service for monitoring and responding to resource state changes in the distributed application.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplication.ResourceCommands
member.summary: Gets the service for executing resource commands.
member.remarks:
Two common use cases for the are:
Progamatically executing resource commands in a running app host.
Unit or integration testing resource commands.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplication.Dispose
member.summary: Disposes the distributed application by disposing the .

remarks.para: Typically developers do not need to worry about calling the Dispose method on the instance because it is typically used in the entry point of the application and all resources used by the application are destroyed when the application exists.

If you are using the and inside unit test code then you should correctly dispose of the instance. This is because the instance initializes configuration providers which make use of file watchers which are a finite resource.

Without disposing of the correctly projects with a large number of functional/integration tests may see a "The configured user limit (128) on the number of inotify instances has been reached, or the per-process limit on the number of open file descriptors has been reached." error.
Refer to the Aspire testing page for more information on how to use Aspire APIs for functional an integrating testing.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplication.DisposeAsync
member.summary: Asynchronously disposes the distributed application by disposing the .

remarks.para: Typically developers do not need to worry about calling the Dispose method on the instance because it is typically used in the entry point of the application and all resources used by the application are destroyed when the application exists.

If you are using the and inside unit test code then you should correctly dispose of the instance. This is because the instance initializes configuration providers which make use of file watchers which are a finite resource.

Without disposing of the correctly projects with a large number of functional/integration tests may see a "The configured user limit (128) on the number of inotify instances has been reached, or the per-process limit on the number of open file descriptors has been reached." error.
Refer to the Aspire testing page for more information on how to use Aspire APIs for functional an integrating testing.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplication.RunAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Runs an application and returns a Task that only completes when the token is triggered or shutdown is triggered and all instances are stopped.
member.param: The token to trigger shutdown.
member.returns: The that represents the asynchronous operation.

remarks.para: When the Aspire app host is launched via there are two possible modes that it is running in:

list.item: Run mode; in run mode the app host runs until a shutdown of the app is triggered either by the users pressing Ctrl-C, the debugger detaching, or the browser associated with the dashboard being closed.
Publish mode; in publish mode the app host runs just long enough to generate a manifest file that is used by deployment tool.

remarks.para: Developers extending the Aspire application model should consider the lifetime of instances which are added to the dependency injection container. For more information on determining the mode that the app host is running in refer to .

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplication.Run
member.summary: Runs an application and blocks the calling thread until host shutdown is triggered and all instances are stopped.

remarks.para: When the Aspire app host is launched via there are two possible modes that it is running in:

list.item: Run mode; in run mode the app host runs until a shutdown of the app is triggered either by the users pressing Ctrl-C, the debugger detaching, or the browser associated with the dashboard being closed.
Publish mode; in publish mode the app host runs just long enough to generate a manifest file that is used by deployment tool.

remarks.para: Developers extending the Aspire application model should consider the lifetime of instances which are added to the dependency injection container. For more information on determining the mode that the app host is running in refer to .

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplication.EnsureComputeEnvironmentAnnotationsApplied(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.DistributedApplicationModel)
member.summary: When the model contains exactly one compute environment, applies a pointing to that environment to every that doesn't already have one.
member.remarks:
This implements the "single compute environment is the default" convention: when only one compute environment is present (e.g., a single AzureContainerAppEnvironmentResource), developers don't need to call WithComputeEnvironment(...) on every compute resource — the unique environment is auto-assigned here. Resources that have been explicitly bound to a compute environment (via WithComputeEnvironment or otherwise) are left untouched.
When zero or more than one compute environment is present we deliberately do nothing. - With zero compute environments there is no default to apply. - With multiple compute environments there is no unambiguous default; the developer must pick one explicitly per resource.
Doing this once here, before the before-start pipeline runs, guarantees downstream consumers (per-environment prepare steps, deployment-target inspection helpers, etc.) see a consistent on every compute resource that has a target environment — without each consumer needing to re-implement the "single env wins" fallback.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplication.ApplyLocaleOverride(Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration,Aspire.Hosting.LocaleOverrideContext)
member.summary: Apply locale from configuration early. At this point it is too early to write to the console so any error from applying the locale is saved to configuration and written once the host is built.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.DistributedApplicationBuilderExtensions.CreateResourceBuilder``1(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new resource builder based on the name of an existing resource.
member.param: The distributed application builder.
The name of an existing resource.

remarks.para: This method is not available in polyglot app hosts.

The method is used to create an for a specific resource where the original resource builder cannot be referenced. This does not create a new resource, but instead returns a resource builder for an existing resource based on its name.

This method is typically used when testing Aspire applications where the original resource builder cannot be referenced directly. Using the method allows for easier mutation of resources within the test scenario.

member.remarks: This method is not available in polyglot app hosts. This method is similar to but returns false instead of throwing an exception when the resource is not found or is not of the correct type.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ExecutableResourceBuilderExtensions.AddExecutable(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String[])
member.summary: Adds an executable resource to the application model.
member.param: The .
The name of the resource.
The executable path. This can be a fully qualified path or a executable to run from the shell/command line.
The working directory of the executable.
The arguments to the executable.
member.remarks:
You can run any executable command using its full path. As a security feature, Aspire doesn't run executable unless the command is located in a path listed in the PATH environment variable.
To run an executable file that's in the current directory, specify the full path or use the relative path ./ to represent the current directory.

member.remarks: When the executable resource is converted to a container resource, the arguments to the executable are not used. member.summary: Adds a health check to the external service resource.
member.param: The external service resource builder.
The relative path to use for the HTTP health check.
The expected HTTP status code for a healthy response. Defaults to 200.

remarks.para: This method adds a health check to the health check service which polls the specified external service on a periodic basis. The address is based on the URL of the external service. A path for the health check request can be specified. The expected status code is set to 200 by default but a different one can be specified.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder
member.summary: A builder for creating instances of .

remarks.para: The is the central interface for defining the resources which are orchestrated by the when the app host is launched.

To create an instance of the interface developers should use the method. Once the builder is created extension methods which target the interface can be used to add resources to the distributed application.

example.para: This example shows a distributed application that contains a .NET project (InventoryService) that uses a Redis cache and a PostgreSQL database. The builder is created using the method.
The AddRedis and AddPostgres methods are used to add Redis and PostgreSQL container resources. The results of the methods are stored in variables for later use.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder.AppHostDirectory
member.summary: Directory of the project where the app host is located. Defaults to the content root if there's no project.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder.AppHostAssembly
member.summary: Assembly of the app host project.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder.Eventing
member.summary: Eventing infrastructure for AppHost lifecycle.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder.ExecutionContext
member.summary: Execution context for this invocation of the AppHost.

remarks.para: The property provides access key information about the context in which the distributed application is running. The most important properties that the provides is the and properties. Developers building Aspire based applications may whish to change the application model depending on whether they are running locally, or whether they are publishing to the cloud.

example.para: An example of using the property on the via the . In this case an extension method is used to generate a stable node name for RabbitMQ for local development runs.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder.Resources
member.summary: Gets the collection of resources for the distributed application.
member.remarks: This can be mutated by adding more resources, which will update its current view.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder.Pipeline
member.summary: Gets the deployment pipeline for this distributed application.
member.remarks: The pipeline allows adding custom deployment steps that execute during the deploy process. Steps can declare dependencies on other steps to control execution order.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder.FileSystemService
member.summary: Gets the service for managing Aspire file system operations.

remarks.para: The provides a centralized way to manage temporary files and directories used by Aspire, enabling testability and consistent temp file management.
Resources and infrastructure code should use this service instead of static methods like or to ensure consistent directory management across the application.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder.UserSecretsManager
member.summary: Gets the service for managing user secrets.

remarks.para: The provides a centralized way to manage user secrets used by Aspire, enabling testability and consistent secret management.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder.AddResource``1(``0)
member.summary: Adds a resource of type to the distributed application.
member.typeparam: The type of resource to add.
member.param: The resource to add.
member.returns: A builder for configuring the added resource.
member.exception: Thrown when a resource with the same name already exists.

remarks.para: The method is not typically used directly by developers building Aspire-based applications. It is typically used by developers building extensions to Aspire and is called from within an extension method to add a custom resource to the application model.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder.CreateResourceBuilder``1(``0)
member.summary: Creates a new resource builder based on an existing resource.
member.param: An existing resource.

remarks.para: The method is used to create an for a specific resource where the original resource builder cannot be referenced. This does not create a new resource, but instead returns a resource builder for an existing resource.

This method is typically used when building extensions to Aspire where the original resource builder cannot be referenced directly. Using the method allows for easier mutation of resources within the application model.

Calling extension methods on the typically results in modifications to the collection. Not all changes to annotations will be effective depending on what stage of the lifecycle the app host is in. See for more details.

example.para: The following example shows the implementation of the extension method.
The method creates a new in the application model. The return type of is . The type does not implement the .

To work around this issue the method wraps the parameter resource in a "surrogate" class which proxies access to the fields but implements . The method assists by allowing the creation of a without adding another resource to the application model.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder.Build
member.summary: Builds and returns a new instance. This can only be called once.

remarks.para: Callers of the method should only call it once. are responsible for the lifecycle of the instance that is returned. Note that the type implements and should be disposed of when it is no longer needed. Note that in many templates and samples Dispose is omitted for brevity because in those cases the instance is destroyed when the process exists.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.MissingParameterValueException
member.summary: The exception that is thrown when a parameter resource cannot be initialized because its value is missing or cannot be resolved.
member.remarks:
This exception is typically thrown when:
A parameter value is not provided in configuration and has no default value
A parameter's value callback throws an exception during execution
A parameter's value cannot be retrieved from the configured source (e.g., user secrets, environment variables)

member.summary: Configures OpenTelemetry in projects using environment variables.
member.param: The resource to add annotations to.
The configuration to use for the OTLP exporter endpoint URL.
The host environment to check if the application is running in development mode.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.OtlpConfigurationExtensions.WithOtlpExporter``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0})
member.summary:
Injects the appropriate environment variables to allow the resource to enable sending telemetry to the dashboard.
It sets the OTLP endpoint to the value of the ASPIRE_DASHBOARD_OTLP_ENDPOINT_URL environment variable.
It sets the service name and instance id to the resource name and UID. Values are injected by the orchestrator.
It sets a small batch schedule delay in development. This reduces the delay that OTLP exporter waits to sends telemetry and makes the dashboard telemetry pages responsive.
member.param: The resource builder.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.OtlpConfigurationExtensions.WithOtlpExporter``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},Aspire.Hosting.OtlpProtocol)
member.summary:
Injects the appropriate environment variables to allow the resource to enable sending telemetry to the dashboard.
It sets the OTLP endpoint to the value of the ASPIRE_DASHBOARD_OTLP_ENDPOINT_URL environment variable.
It sets the service name and instance id to the resource name and UID. Values are injected by the orchestrator.
It sets a small batch schedule delay in development. This reduces the delay that OTLP exporter waits to sends telemetry and makes the dashboard telemetry pages responsive.
member.param: The resource builder.
The protocol to use for the OTLP exporter. If not set, it will try gRPC then Http.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.OtlpConfigurationExtensions.ResolveOtlpEndpointFromDashboard(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EnvironmentCallbackContext,System.Nullable{Aspire.Hosting.OtlpProtocol})
member.summary: Tries to resolve the OTLP endpoint from the dashboard resource in the distributed application model. This ensures that when ports are randomized (e.g. isolated mode), resources use the actual allocated endpoint rather than the statically configured port.
member.remarks: The returned has no network context baked in, so it resolves using the calling resource's network at evaluation time. This means containers automatically get container-network URLs and non-containers get localhost URLs.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.DistributedApplicationPipeline.Clone
member.summary: Creates a clone of this pipeline whose built-in steps are independent copies (with fresh / lists). Configuration callbacks are shallow-copied — the same delegates are reused.
member.remarks: Used by DistributedApplication to run the BeforeStart phase against a throwaway pipeline instance so that step-graph mutations performed by (e.g. appending entries to on built-in steps) do not leak into the singleton pipeline used later for publish.

The pipeline context for execution.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.DistributedApplicationPipeline.ResolveStepsAsync(Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineContext)
member.summary: Resolves all pipeline steps (from built-in steps and resource annotations), normalizes RequiredBy relationships to DependsOn, and validates the steps without executing them. The returned list is in collection order; use to obtain execution order.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.DistributedApplicationPipeline.NormalizeRequiredByToDependsOn(System.Collections.Generic.List{Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineStep},System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineStep})
member.summary: Converts all RequiredBy relationships to their equivalent DependsOn relationships. If step A is required by step B, this adds step A as a dependency of step B.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.DistributedApplicationPipeline.ExecuteStepsAsTaskDag(System.Collections.Generic.List{Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineStep},System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineStep},Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineContext)
member.summary: Executes pipeline steps by building a Task DAG where each step waits on its dependencies. Failed steps prevent dependent steps from executing, while independent steps continue running.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.DistributedApplicationPipeline.ExecuteStepsSequentially(System.Collections.Generic.List{Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineStep},Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineContext)
member.summary: Executes pipeline steps sequentially in topological order. Each step waits for the previous step to complete before starting.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.DistributedApplicationPipeline.VisitState
member.summary: Represents the visitation state of a step during cycle detection.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.DistributedApplicationPipeline.VisitState.Unvisited
member.summary: The step has not been visited yet.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.DistributedApplicationPipeline.VisitState.Visiting
member.summary: The step is currently being visited (on the current DFS path).

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.DistributedApplicationPipeline.VisitState.Visited
member.summary: The step has been fully visited (all descendants explored).

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.DistributedApplicationPipeline.ValidateDependencyGraph(System.Collections.Generic.List{Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineStep},System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineStep})
member.summary: Validates that the pipeline steps form a directed acyclic graph (DAG) with no circular dependencies.
member.remarks:
Uses depth-first search (DFS) to detect cycles. A cycle exists if we encounter a node that is currently being visited (in the Visiting state), meaning we've found a back edge in the graph.
Example: A → B → C is valid (no cycle) Example: A → B → C → A is invalid (cycle detected) Example: A → B, A → C, B → D, C → D is valid (diamond dependency, no cycle)

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.DistributedApplicationPipeline.DumpDependencyGraphDiagnostics(System.Collections.Generic.List{Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineStep},Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineStepContext)
member.summary: Dumps comprehensive diagnostic information about the dependency graph, including reasons why certain steps may not be executed.

member.remarks:
Pattern:
^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match a character in the set [-0-9A-Z_a-z] atomically at least once.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.summary: Provides loggers that forward calls to a contextual logger associated with the current pipeline step.
member.remarks: This logger provider uses AsyncLocal storage to maintain the current logger context across async operations. This enables pipeline steps to log through a contextual logger that can be set per execution.

member.summary: Holds the logger instance in AsyncLocal storage.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineLoggerProvider.StepLogger
member.summary: A logger implementation that forwards all calls to the current contextual logger.
member.remarks: This logger acts as a proxy and dynamically resolves the current logger on each operation, allowing the target logger to change between calls. When logging exceptions, stack traces are only included when the configured minimum log level is Debug or Trace.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineStepHelpers.PushImageToRegistryAsync(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResource,Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineStepContext)
member.summary: Pushes a container image to a registry with proper logging and task reporting. If the registry is a local registry (empty endpoint), only tags the image without pushing. If the image format is OCI (or other non-Docker format), this is a no-op since OCI images are typically written to local file paths and don't require a push to a registry.
member.param: The resource whose image should be pushed.
The pipeline step context for logging and task reporting.
member.returns: A task representing the async operation.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineSummary
member.summary: Represents pipeline summary information to be displayed after pipeline completion. This is a general-purpose key-value collection that pipeline steps can contribute to.

remarks.para: This class provides a flexible way for any pipeline step to contribute information to be displayed after pipeline execution. The data is stored as key-value pairs that will be formatted as a table or list in the CLI output.

Pipeline steps can add any relevant information such as resource group names, subscription IDs, URLs, namespaces, cluster names, or any other details. Values can be plain text or Markdown-formatted by using .
The summary is available via the property and can be accessed from any pipeline step.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.Pipelines.PipelineSummary.Items
member.summary: Gets the items in the pipeline summary as a read-only collection. Items are displayed in the order they were added.

member.summary: Adds a key-value pair to the pipeline summary with a plain-text value.
member.param: The key or label for the item (e.g., "Namespace", "URL").
The plain-text value for the item.

member.summary: Adds a key-value pair to the pipeline summary with a Markdown-formatted value.
member.param: The key or label for the item (e.g., "Namespace", "URL").
The Markdown-formatted value for the item.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ProjectResourceBuilderExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for to add and configure project resources.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ProjectResourceBuilderExtensions.AddProject``1(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a .NET project to the application model.
member.typeparam: A type that represents the project reference.
member.param: The .
The name of the resource. This name will be used for service discovery when referenced in a dependency.

remarks.para: This overload of the method takes a type parameter. The type parameter is constrained to types that implement the interface.

Classes that implement the interface are generated when a .NET project is added as a reference to the app host project. The generated class contains a property that returns the path to the referenced project file. Using this path Aspire parses the launchSettings.json file to determine which launch profile to use when running the project, and what endpoint configuration to automatically generate.

The name of the automatically generated project metadata type is a normalized version of the project name. Periods, dashes, and spaces in project names are converted to underscores. This normalization may lead to naming conflicts. If a conflict occurs the <ProjectReference /> that references the project can have a AspireProjectMetadataTypeName="..." attribute added to override the name.
Note that endpoints coming from the Kestrel configuration are automatically added to the project. The Kestrel Url and Protocols are used to build the equivalent .
remarks.remarks: This method is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use the overload with projectPath instead.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ProjectResourceBuilderExtensions.AddProject(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a .NET project to the application model.
member.param: The .
The name of the resource. This name will be used for service discovery when referenced in a dependency.
The path to the project file.

remarks.para: This overload of the method adds a project to the application model using a path to the project file. This allows for projects to be referenced that may not be part of the same solution. If the project path is not an absolute path then it will be computed relative to the app host directory.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ProjectResourceBuilderExtensions.AddProject``1(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,System.String)

member.param: The .
The name of the resource. This name will be used for service discovery when referenced in a dependency.
The launch profile to use. If null then no launch profile will be used.

The name of the automatically generated project metadata type is a normalized version of the project name. Periods, dashes, and spaces in project names are converted to underscores. This normalization may lead to naming conflicts. If a conflict occurs the <ProjectReference /> that references the project can have a AspireProjectMetadataTypeName="..." attribute added to override the name.
remarks.remarks: This method is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use the overload with projectPath instead.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ProjectResourceBuilderExtensions.WithReplicas(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ProjectResource},System.Int32)
member.summary: Configures how many replicas of the project should be created for the project.
member.param: The project resource builder.
The number of replicas.

remarks.para: When this method is applied to a project resource it will configure the app host to start multiple instances of the application based on the specified number of replicas. By default the app host automatically starts a reverse proxy for each process. When is used the reverse proxy will load balance traffic between the replicas.
This capability can be useful when debugging scale out scenarios to ensure state is appropriately managed within a cluster of instances.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ProjectResourceBuilderExtensions.DisableForwardedHeaders(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ProjectResource})
member.summary: Configures the project to disable forwarded headers when being published.
member.param: The project resource builder.

remarks.para: By default Aspire assumes that .NET applications which expose endpoints should be configured to use forwarded headers. This is because most typical cloud native deployment scenarios involve a reverse proxy which translates an external endpoint hostname to an internal address.
To enable forwarded headers the ASPNETCORE_FORWARDEDHEADERS_ENABLED variable is injected into the project and set to true. If the extension is used this environment variable will not be set.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ProjectResourceBuilderExtensions.WithEndpointsInEnvironment(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ProjectResource},System.Func{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointAnnotation,System.Boolean})
member.summary: Set a filter that determines if environment variables are injected for a given endpoint. By default, all endpoints are included (if this method is not called).
member.param: The project resource builder.
The filter callback that returns true if and only if the endpoint should be included.
member.remarks: This method is not available in polyglot app hosts.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ProjectResourceBuilderExtensions.PublishAsDockerFile``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.Action{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ContainerResource}})
member.summary: Adds support for containerizing this during deployment. The resulting container image is built, and when the optional action is provided, it is used to configure the container resource.
member.remarks: When the executable resource is converted to a container resource, the arguments to the executable are not used. This is because arguments to the project often contain physical paths that are not valid in the container. The container can be set up with the correct arguments using the action.
member.param: Resource builder
Optional action to configure the container resource

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.RequiredCommandResourceExtensions.WithRequiredCommand``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Declares that a resource requires a specific command/executable to be available on the local machine PATH before it can start.
member.param: The resource builder.
The command string (file name or path) that should be validated.
An optional help link URL to guide users when the command is missing.
member.remarks: The command is considered valid if either: 1. It is an absolute or relative path (contains a directory separator) that points to an existing file, or 2. It is discoverable on the current process PATH (respecting PATHEXT on Windows). If the command is not found, a warning message will be logged but the resource will be allowed to attempt to start.

member.summary: Declares that a resource requires a specific command/executable to be available on the local machine PATH before it can start, with custom validation logic.
member.param: The resource builder.
The command string (file name or path) that should be validated.
A callback that validates the resolved command path. Receives a and returns a .
An optional help link URL to guide users when the command is missing or fails validation.
member.remarks: This method is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use the overload without validation callback instead. The command is first resolved to a full path. If found, the validation callback is invoked with the context containing the resolved path and service provider. The callback should return a indicating whether the command is valid. If the command is not found or fails validation, a warning message will be logged but the resource will be allowed to attempt to start.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ResourceBuilderExtensions.WithReference``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceWithConnectionString},System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Injects a connection string as an environment variable from the source resource into the destination resource, using the source resource's name as the connection string name (if not overridden). The format of the environment variable will be "ConnectionStrings__{sourceResourceName}={connectionString}".
Each resource defines the format of the connection string value. The underlying connection string value can be retrieved using .
Connection strings are also resolved by the configuration system (appSettings.json in the AppHost project, or environment variables). If a connection string is not found on the resource, the configuration system will be queried for a connection string using the resource's name.
member.param: The resource where connection string will be injected.
The resource from which to extract the connection string.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ResourceBuilderExtensions.WithReference``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.IResourceWithServiceDiscovery})
member.summary: Injects service discovery and endpoint information as environment variables from the source resource into the destination resource, using the source resource's name as the service name. Each non-excluded endpoint (where is false) defined on the source resource will be injected using the format defined by the on the destination resource, i.e. either "services__{sourceResourceName}__{endpointScheme}__{endpointIndex}={uriString}" for .NET service discovery, or "{RESOURCE_ENDPOINT}={uri}" for endpoint injection.
member.param: The resource where the service discovery information will be injected.
The resource from which to extract service discovery information.

remarks.para:
All endpoints are included in the default reference set unless explicitly excluded. Resource authors can opt out individual endpoints by setting to true (for example, using .WithEndpoint("endpointName", e => e.ExcludeReferenceEndpoint = true)) to exclude them from this method's behavior. Endpoints that have been excluded (such as management or health check endpoints) can still be referenced explicitly using
with .

Create endpoint if it does not exist.

remarks.para: The method allows developers to mutate any aspect of an endpoint annotation. Note that changing one value does not automatically change other values to compatible/consistent values. For example setting the property of the endpoint annotation in the callback will not automatically change the . All values should be set in the callback if the defaults are not acceptable.
This method is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use the callback-based endpoint mutation export instead.

Network protocol: TCP or UDP are supported today, others possibly in future.

The callback will be executed after endpoints have been allocated and the URL has been generated.
This allows you to modify the URL or its display text.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ResourceBuilderExtensions.WaitFor``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResource})
member.summary: Waits for the dependency resource to enter the Running state before starting the resource.
member.param: The resource builder for the resource that will be waiting.
The resource builder for the dependency resource.

remarks.para: This method is useful when a resource should wait until another has started running. This can help reduce errors in logs during local development where dependency resources.

Some resources automatically register health checks with the application host container. For these resources, calling also results in the resource being blocked from starting until the health checks associated with the dependency resource return .
The method can be used to associate additional health checks with a resource.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ResourceBuilderExtensions.WaitFor``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResource},Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.WaitBehavior)
member.summary: Waits for the dependency resource to enter the Running state before starting the resource.
member.param: The resource builder for the resource that will be waiting.
The resource builder for the dependency resource.
The wait behavior to use.

remarks.para: This method is useful when a resource should wait until another has started running. This can help reduce errors in logs during local development where dependency resources.

Some resources automatically register health checks with the application host container. For these resources, calling also results in the resource being blocked from starting until the health checks associated with the dependency resource return .
The method can be used to associate additional health checks with a resource.

The parameter can be used to control the behavior of the wait operation. When is specified, the wait operation will continue to wait until the resource becomes healthy. This is the default behavior with the overload.
When is specified, the wait operation will throw a if the resource enters an unavailable state.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ResourceBuilderExtensions.WaitForStart``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResource})
member.summary: Waits for the dependency resource to enter the Running state before starting the resource.
member.param: The resource builder for the resource that will be waiting.
The resource builder for the dependency resource.

remarks.para: This method is useful when a resource should wait until another has started running but doesn't need to wait for health checks to pass. This can help enable initialization scenarios where services need to start before health checks can pass.
Unlike , this method only waits for the dependency resource to enter the Running state and ignores any health check annotations associated with the dependency resource.

Unlike , this method only waits for the dependency resource to enter the Running state and ignores any health check annotations associated with the dependency resource.

The parameter can be used to control the behavior of the wait operation. When is specified, the wait operation will continue to wait until the resource enters the Running state. This is the default behavior with the overload.
When is specified, the wait operation will throw a if the resource enters an unavailable state.

member.summary: Adds a to the resource annotations to add a resource command.
member.param: The resource builder.
The name of the command. The name uniquely identifies the command.
The display name visible in UI.
A callback that is executed when the command is executed. The callback is run inside the Aspire host. The callback result is used to indicate success or failure in the UI.
Optional configuration for the command.

remarks.para: The WithCommand method is used to add commands to the resource. Commands are displayed in the dashboard and can be executed by a user using the dashboard UI.
When a command is executed, the callback is called and is run inside the Aspire host.

member.summary: Adds a to the resource annotations to add a resource command.
member.param: The resource builder.
The name of the command. The name uniquely identifies the command.
The display name visible in UI.
A callback that is executed when the command is executed. The callback is run inside the Aspire host. The callback result is used to indicate success or failure in the UI.

param.name: updateState
param.para: A callback that is used to update the command state. The callback is executed when the command's resource snapshot is updated.
If a callback isn't specified, the command is always enabled.

member.param: Optional description of the command, to be shown in the UI. Could be used as a tooltip. May be localized.
Optional parameter that configures the command in some way. Clients must return any value provided by the server when invoking the command.
When a confirmation message is specified, the UI will prompt with an OK/Cancel dialog and the confirmation message before starting the command.
The icon name for the command. The name should be a valid FluentUI icon name from
The icon variant.
A flag indicating whether the command is highlighted in the UI.

remarks.para: The WithCommand method is used to add commands to the resource. Commands are displayed in the dashboard and can be executed by a user using the dashboard UI.
When a command is executed, the callback is called and is run inside the Aspire host.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ResourceBuilderExtensions.WithHttpCommand``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.HttpCommandOptions)
member.summary: Adds a command to the resource that when invoked sends an HTTP request to the specified endpoint and path.
member.param: The resource builder.
The path to send the request to when the command is invoked.
The display name visible in UI.
The name of the HTTP endpoint on this resource to send the request to when the command is invoked.
Optional name of the command. The name uniquely identifies the command. If a name isn't specified then it's inferred using the command's endpoint and HTTP method.
Optional configuration for the command.

remarks.para: The command will be added to the resource represented by .

If is specified, the request will be sent to the endpoint with that name on the resource represented by . If an endpoint with that name is not found, or the endpoint with that name is not an HTTP endpoint, an exception will be thrown.

If no is specified, the first HTTP endpoint found on the resource will be used. HTTP endpoints with an https scheme are preferred over those with an http scheme. If no HTTP endpoint is found on the resource, an exception will be thrown.
The command will not be enabled until the endpoint is allocated and the resource the endpoint is associated with is healthy.
If is not specified, POST will be used.
Specifying will use that named when sending the request. This allows you to configure the instance with a specific handler or other options using . If is not specified, the default will be used.
The callback will be invoked to configure the request before it is sent. This can be used to add headers or a request payload before the request is sent.

The callback will be invoked after the response is received to determine the result of the command invocation. If this callback is not specified, the command will be considered successful if the response status code is in the 2xx range. Set to flow the HTTP response body back to the command caller.
This method is not available in polyglot app hosts.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ResourceBuilderExtensions.WithHttpCommand``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},System.String,System.String,System.Func{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.EndpointReference},System.String,Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.HttpCommandOptions)
member.summary: Adds a command to the resource that when invoked sends an HTTP request to the specified endpoint and path.
member.param: The resource builder.
The path to send the request to when the command is invoked.
The display name visible in UI.
A callback that selects the HTTP endpoint to send the request to when the command is invoked.
Optional configuration for the command.
The name of command. The name uniquely identifies the command.

remarks.para: The command will be added to the resource represented by .

If no is specified, the first HTTP endpoint found on the resource will be used. HTTP endpoints with an https scheme are preferred over those with an http scheme. If no HTTP endpoint is found on the resource, an exception will be thrown.
The supplied may return an endpoint from a different resource to that which the command is being added to.
The command will not be enabled until the endpoint is allocated and the resource the endpoint is associated with is healthy.
If is not specified, POST will be used.
Specifying a will use that named when sending the request. This allows you to configure the instance with a specific handler or other options using . If no is specified, the default will be used.
The callback will be invoked to configure the request before it is sent. This can be used to add headers or a request payload before the request is sent.

The callback will be invoked after the response is received to determine the result of the command invocation. If this callback is not specified, the command will be considered successful if the response status code is in the 2xx range. Set to flow the HTTP response body back to the command caller.
This method is not available in polyglot app hosts.

member.summary: Adds a to the resource annotations to associate a certificate authority collection with the resource. This is used to configure additional trusted certificate authorities for the resource. Custom certificate trust is only applied in run mode; in publish mode resources will use their default certificate trust behavior.

Currently this indicates trust for the ASP.NET Core developer certificate. The developer certificate will only be trusted when running in local development scenarios; in publish mode resources will use their default certificate trust.
member.param: The resource builder.

Custom certificate trust is only applied in run mode; in publish mode resources will use their default certificate trust behavior.
member.param: The resource builder.
The scope to apply to custom certificate authorities associated with the resource.

member.summary: Adds a to the resource annotations to associate a callback that is invoked when a resource needs to configure itself for custom certificate trust. member.summary: Indicates that a resource should use the developer certificate key pair for HTTPS endpoints at run time. Currently this indicates use of the ASP.NET Core developer certificate. The developer certificate will only be used when running in local development scenarios; in publish mode resources will use their default certificate configuration.
member.param: The resource builder.
A parameter specifying the password used to encrypt the certificate private key.

remarks.example:
Use the developer certificate for HTTPS/TLS endpoints on a container resource:
builder.AddContainer("my-service", "my-image") .WithHttpsDeveloperCertificate()

Use this overload when the callback needs to perform asynchronous operations such as retrieving configuration values from external sources. The callback receives a that provides access to the resource and the that can be modified. Multiple callbacks can be registered on the same resource, and they will be invoked in the order they were added.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.IFileSystemService.TempDirectory
member.summary: Gets the temporary directory service for managing temporary files and directories.

remarks.para: Use this instead of calling directly to ensure consistent temp file management and enable testability.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ITempFileSystemService
member.summary: Service for managing temporary directories and files within Aspire.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ITempFileSystemService.CreateTempSubdirectory(System.String)
member.summary: Creates and returns a temporary subdirectory.
member.param: Optional prefix for the subdirectory name (e.g., "aspire").
member.returns: A representing the created temporary subdirectory. Dispose to delete.

remarks.para: This method creates a unique temporary subdirectory using the system temp folder. The directory is created immediately. Dispose the returned object to delete the directory.
Use this instead of calling directly.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ITempFileSystemService.CreateTempFile(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new temporary file and returns it.
member.param: Optional file name for the temporary file (e.g., "config.json", "script.sh"). If null, uses a random name.
member.returns: A representing the created temporary file. Dispose to delete.

remarks.para: This method creates a new temporary file. If a file name is specified, it creates a temporary subdirectory and places the file with that name inside it. If no file name is specified, it uses . Dispose the returned object to delete the file.
Use this instead of calling directly.

member.summary: Attempts to discover DCP from a base directory. Checks for the expected bundle layout structure.

member.name: T:Aspire.Shared.ContainerRuntimeDetector

member.name: M:Aspire.Shared.ContainerRuntimeDetector.FindAvailableRuntimeAsync(System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Finds the best available container runtime, optionally using an explicit preference.
member.param: An explicitly configured runtime name (e.g., "docker" or "podman" from ASPIRE_CONTAINER_RUNTIME). When set, only that runtime is checked. When null, all known runtimes are probed in parallel.
Optional logger for diagnostic output during detection.
Cancellation token.
member.returns: The best available runtime, or null if no runtime was found. When a runtime is configured but not available, returns its info with = false.

member.summary: Selects the best runtime from pre-probed results using DCP's priority logic. Use this when you've already probed runtimes and want to determine which one to use.

member.name: M:Aspire.Shared.ConsoleLogs.TimestampParser.GenerateRfc3339RegEx
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^ # Starts the string\r\n(\\d{4}) # Four digits for the year\r\n- # Separator for the date\r\n(0[1-9]|1[0-2]) # Two digits for the month, restricted to 01-12\r\n- # Separator for the date\r\n(0[1-9]|[12][0-9]|3[01]) # Two digits for the day, restricted to 01-31\r\nT # Literal, separator between date and time, either a T or a space\r\n([01][0-9]|2[0-3]) # Two digits for the hour, restricted to 00-23\r\n: # Separator for the time\r\n([0-5][0-9]) # Two digits for the minutes, restricted to 00-59\r\n: # Separator for the time\r\n([0-5][0-9]) # Two digits for the seconds, restricted to 00-59\r\n(\\.\\d{1,9})? # A period and up to nine digits for the partial seconds (optional)\r\n(Z|([Z+-]([01][0-9]|2[0-3]):?([0-5][0-9])))? # Time Zone offset, in the form Z or ZHH:MM or ZHHMM or +HH:MM or +HHMM or -HH:MM or -HHMM (optional)
Options:
RegexOptions.ExplicitCapture | RegexOptions.IgnorePatternWhitespace | RegexOptions.CultureInvariant
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match a Unicode digit exactly 4 times.
○ Match '-'.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match '0'.
○ Match a character in the set [1-9].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match '1'.
○ Match a character in the set [0-2].
○ Match '-'.
○ Match with 3 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match '0'.
○ Match a character in the set [1-9].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [12].
○ Match a character in the set [0-9].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match '3'.
○ Match a character in the set [01].
○ Match 'T'.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [01].
○ Match a character in the set [0-9].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match '2'.
○ Match a character in the set [0-3].
○ Match ':'.
○ Match a character in the set [0-5].
○ Match a character in the set [0-9].
○ Match ':'.
○ Match a character in the set [0-5].
○ Match a character in the set [0-9].
○ Optional (greedy).
○ Match '.'.
○ Match a Unicode digit greedily at least 1 and at most 9 times.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions, atomically.
○ Match 'Z'.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [+-Z].
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [01].
○ Match a character in the set [0-9].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match '2'.
○ Match a character in the set [0-3].
○ Match ':' atomically, optionally.
○ Match a character in the set [0-5].
○ Match a character in the set [0-9].

member.name: M:Aspire.ChannelExtensions.GetBatchesAsync``1(System.Threading.Channels.Channel{``0},System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Reads batches, grabbing all available messages and returning them as one batch before yielding. This can give a better downstream experience, as there's less per-item overhead.
member.remarks: This sequence adopts the lifetime of . Callers are required to either use a channel that will complete, or to pass a cancellation token which will also cancel the sequence returned by this method.
member.typeparam: The type of items in the channel and returned batch.
member.param: The channel to read values from.
The minimum read interval. The enumerable will wait this long before returning the next available result.
A token that signals a loss of interest in the operation.

member.name: T:Aspire.CircularBuffer`1
member.summary: The circular buffer starts with an empty list and grows to a maximum size. When the buffer is full, adding or inserting a new item removes the first item in the buffer.

member.summary: Gets the named configuration value as a boolean.
member.remarks: Parses true and false, along with 1 and 0.
member.param: The this method extends.
The configuration key.
A default value, for when the configuration value is unspecified or white space.

member.name: M:Aspire.IConfigurationExtensions.GetUri(Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration,System.String,System.Uri)
member.summary: Parses a configuration value into a object.
member.param: The this method extends.
The configuration key.
A default value, for when the configuration value is unspecified or white space. May be .
member.returns: The parsed value, or the default value if specified and parsing failed. Returns if is and parsing failed.
member.exception: The configuration value could not be accessed, or contained incorrectly formatted data.

member.name: M:Aspire.IConfigurationExtensions.GetUris(Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration,System.String,System.Uri)
member.summary: Parses a configuration value's semicolon-delimited value into an array of objects.
member.param: The this method extends.
The configuration key.
A default value, for when the configuration value is unspecified or white space. May be .
member.returns: The parsed values, or the default value if specified and parsing failed. Returns if is and parsing failed.
member.exception: The configuration value could not be accessed, or contained incorrectly formatted data.

member.name: M:Aspire.IConfigurationExtensions.GetEnum``1(Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration,System.String,System.Nullable{``0})
member.summary: Gets the named configuration value as a member of an enum, or if the value was null or empty.
member.remarks: Parsing is case-insensitive.
member.param: The this method extends.
The configuration key.
A default value, for when the configuration value is unable to be parsed.
member.exception: The configuration value is not a valid member of the enum.
member.returns: The parsed enum member, or the configuration value was null or empty.

member.name: M:Aspire.IConfigurationExtensions.GetEnum``1(Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration,System.String)
member.summary: Gets the specified required configuration value as a member of an enum.
member.remarks: Parsing is case-insensitive.
member.param: The this method extends.
The configuration key.
member.exception: The configuration value is empty or not a valid member of the enum.

member.name: M:Aspire.IConfigurationExtensions.GetValueWithNormalizedKey(Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfiguration,System.String)
member.summary: Gets a configuration value with support for dash-to-underscore normalization. First tries the exact configuration key, then tries with dashes replaced by underscores.
member.remarks: This supports command-line arguments and environment variables where dashes are replaced with underscores. For example, a parameter named "my-param" can be resolved from configuration key "my_param".
member.param: The this method extends.
The configuration key to look up.
member.returns: The configuration value, or if not found.

member.name: T:PathLookupHelper
member.summary: Provides helper methods for looking up executables on the system PATH.

member.name: M:PathLookupHelper.FindFullPathFromPath(System.String)
member.summary: Finds the full path of a command by searching the system PATH. On Windows, this also searches for executables with common extensions (.exe, .cmd, .bat, etc.).
member.param: The command name to search for.
member.returns: The full path to the executable if found; otherwise, null.

member.name: M:PathLookupHelper.FindFullPathFromPath(System.String,System.String,System.Char,System.Func{System.String,System.Boolean},System.String[])
member.summary: Finds the full path of a command by searching the specified PATH variable.
member.param: The command name to search for.
The PATH environment variable value to search.
The character used to separate paths in the PATH variable.
A function to check if a file exists at a given path.
Optional array of executable extensions to try (e.g., .exe, .cmd). When provided, these extensions will be appended to the command if not already present.
member.returns: The full path to the executable if found; otherwise, null.
